eligibility.geographic_restrictions: US only. Projects must take place within the US.
eligibility.org_type_requirements: 501(c)(3) nonprofits, municipal/state governments, school districts, tribal entities, community/veterans hospitals. One third-party source (GrantsBuddy) explicitly lists private foundations as ineligible; Reeve's own materials say '501(c)(3)' without distinguishing public charity vs private foundation.
eligibility.other_restrictions: Must serve people living with PARALYSIS (spinal cord injury, stroke, spina bifida, MS, cerebral palsy, ALS). Will NOT fund: research, clinical trials, rehab therapy, major construction, DME (with exceptions), financial assistance to individuals, fundraising events, lobbying.

grant_tiers.Tier 2 - Priority Impact: Up to $30,000 (12 months, fall only, assistive tech/disaster/respite)
grant_tiers.Tier 3 - Priority Impact: Up to $40,000 (18 months, fall only, rural populations)
grant_tiers.Tier 4 - Priority Impact: Up to $50,000 (24 months, fall only, employment/nursing home transition)
grant_tiers.Tier 5 - Expanded Impact: Up to $100,000 (24 months, spring only, previous grantees only)

deadlines: March 12, 2026 (1st Cycle proposals due). Award announcement May 30, 2026.
funding_cycle: semi-annual (spring: Direct Effect + Expanded Impact; fall: Direct Effect + Priority Impact)
recent_grantees_listed: High Fives Foundation ($24,549 - Adaptive Mountain Bike Programming)
BORP / Bay Area Outreach & Recreation Program (Adaptive Sports, Bay Area)
fit_notes: Strong program fit: adaptive sports is core funded area; veterans receive 'special consideration'; BORP (Bay Area) has received QOL grants confirming Bay Area adaptive rec funding; Achieve Tahoe $25K confirms this exact type of work is funded. Two major risks: (1) PF eligibility unclear, (2) must demonstrate VetsBoats serves veterans with PARALYSIS specifically, not just veterans generally.
flags_for_alec: 1) PF ELIGIBILITY: Must confirm with Reeve (QOL@Reeve.org) whether 501(c)(3) private operating foundation is eligible. GrantsBuddy lists PFs as ineligible; Reeve's own materials are ambiguous. 2) PARALYSIS REQUIREMENT: Application must frame program around veterans with spinal cord injury/paralysis, not veterans generally. 3) DEADLINE IMMINENT: March 12, 2026. 4) Reeve is a public charity (990 filer), not in our 990-PF data.

application_method: online portal (Nonprofit Central profile + program-specific forms)

eligibility.geographic_restrictions: Orange County, CA only. Must serve or be headquartered in Orange County.
eligibility.org_type_requirements: 501(c)(3) Public Charity only. FAQ explicitly states: 'Organizations with a Private Foundation designation are currently not eligible.'
eligibility.budget_size_requirements: Not stated for most programs. TK Foundation Youth Sailing: grant cannot exceed 25% of agency annual budget.
eligibility.other_restrictions: Must complete Nonprofit Central profile. Previous/current grantees must have all reporting current. Unsolicited proposals not accepted.

grant_size_range: $4,000 - $15,000 (TK Foundation Youth Sailing); other programs not publicly stated
deadlines: TK Youth Sailing: Jan 31, 2026 (CLOSED). Arts funds: opens March 16, 2026. Warne Endangered Species: opens Sep 2025.
fit_notes: DISQUALIFIED on two independent fatal criteria: (1) private foundations are explicitly ineligible, (2) geographic scope is OC-only, VetsBoats is SF Bay. The TK Foundation Youth Sailing Program administered through OCCF has broader geography (North America) but PF eligibility is still likely blocked and it emphasizes youth, not veterans.
flags_for_alec: NO GO. Double disqualifier: PF ineligible + OC-only geography. Side note: TK Foundation (thetkfoundation.org) may warrant separate research if VetsBoats has youth-serving programs, but PF eligibility is still likely a blocker.

application_method: online portal (Blackbaud YourCause). Enter Tax ID, complete eligibility quiz, submit brief proposal. Foundation responds within two months.

eligibility.geographic_restrictions: None. National scope, funds across all states.
eligibility.org_type_requirements: 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Must have filed Form 990-EZ or 990 for past two fiscal years (NOTE: this language excludes 990-PF filers).
eligibility.budget_size_requirements: Two years of gross receipts exceeding $50,000. Must provide audited or certified financial statements.
eligibility.other_restrictions: Programs must exclusively serve service members, veterans, their families, and caregivers. Must provide direct services (not research-only or advocacy-only). Must employ professional staff. Prefers not to be sole/lead funder.

program_areas_funded: Rehabilitation and recovery (mental health, TBI, substance abuse)
Quality of life (adaptive sports, housing, food insecurity, legal services)
deadlines: Rolling. Applications accepted and reviewed year-round.
recent_grantees_listed: Move United ($2.5M+ total, adaptive sports at military medical centers)
Disabled Sports USA ($100,000, sports rehabilitation for 300 disabled veterans in 15 states)
Great Lakes Adaptive Sports Association (Got Your 6 partner)
fit_notes: HIGHEST programmatic fit of all 5 candidates. BWF funds adaptive sports for veterans as a core program area. Strong track record with Move United, Disabled Sports USA. Water sports (kayaking, surfing, sailing) explicitly listed under funded Disabled Sports USA programs. Rolling applications, $5K-$500K range. HOWEVER: eligibility requires 990/990-EZ filing, not 990-PF. VetsBoats files 990-PF exclusively.
flags_for_alec: 1) CRITICAL: 990-PF vs 990/990-EZ. BWF requires '990EZ or 990 for last two fiscal years.' VetsBoats files 990-PF. Could be hard disqualifier. Recommend contacting grants@bobwoodrufffoundation.org or attempting portal eligibility quiz with EIN 464194065. 2) EIN MISMATCH: Prompt listed EIN 237047543, but BWF's actual EIN is 261441650. EIN 237047543 not found in DB. 3) Budget check: BWF requires $50K+ gross receipts for 2 years. VetsBoats assets grew from $69K (2018) to $353K (2023), but gross receipts need verification. 4) BWF prefers not to be sole funder.

filer_name: Shirley Ryan AbilityLab (fka Rehabilitation Institute of Chicago)
fit_notes: SKIP. SRAlab is a hospital ($447M revenue, $1.27B assets), not a grantmaker. The $6.1M in 'grants' on Cause IQ are research subcontracts to universities (Northwestern, U of Illinois), not philanthropic grants. The Judd Goldman Adaptive Sailing connection is a referral partnership: SRAlab refers patients to JGASF, does not fund them. JGASF is independently funded by Chicago-area family foundations. Zero grantmaking pathway for VetsBoats.
flags_for_alec: SKIP. Not a grantmaker. Judd Goldman connection is clinical referral, not funding.

application_method: Annual call for projects (2026 cycle CLOSED)

eligibility.geographic_restrictions: Strictly Lake Tahoe Basin (CA/NV). Projects must be located in the Tahoe Basin and contribute to TRPA environmental thresholds.
eligibility.other_restrictions: Projects must contribute to TRPA thresholds: water quality, air quality, soil conservation, scenic quality, recreation, fisheries, wildlife, noise, vegetation.

program_areas_funded: Environmental improvement in Lake Tahoe region
deadlines: 2026 Call for Projects: CLOSED. Decisions expected end of March 2026.
fit_notes: SKIP. Strictly Tahoe Basin environmental fund. The $100K to Achieve Tahoe makes sense because Achieve Tahoe runs adaptive sports programs IN the Tahoe region. VetsBoats has zero geographic overlap (SF Bay) and zero programmatic overlap (veteran services vs environmental conservation).
flags_for_alec: SKIP. Tahoe-only geography + environmental mission. No fit for SF Bay veteran adaptive sailing.
